Ortiz gang.description: An illegalist anarchist group active in Western Europe in the early 1890s, composed of French and Italian militants. Formed around Léon Ortiz, the gang committed a series of burglaries and played a central role in the Trial of the Thirty. Its members were convicted for theft and penal servitude.

descriptions: The Ortiz Gang followed and was influenced by the Intransigeants of London and Paris.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang is known for its series of burglaries, its influence on the emergence and development of illegalism, following Vittorio Pini's Intransigents of London and Paris

descriptions: The Continuity IRA is a splinter group that split from the Provisional IRA.
edges.evidence_quote: Several splinter groups have been formed as a result of splits within the IRA, including the Continuity IRA , which is still active in the dissident Irish republican campaign , and the Real IRA .

descriptions: The Real IRA and Continuity IRA have cooperated locally.
edges.evidence_quote: The RIRA is distinct from the Continuity IRA, another Provisional IRA splinter group founded in 1986, although the two groups have been known to co-operate at a local level.

Hopi Boys.description: The Hopi Boys are an Afro-Curaçaoan criminal group based in the south-east region of Amsterdam, Netherlands. They are one of the most influential gangs in the Dutch capital, controlling rackets, drug dealing, and prostitution. The group is predominantly Afro-Curaçaoan and has allies such as the Green gang, while rivaling the Kloekhorststraat Gang.

edges.evidence_quote: The Amsterdam district Bijlmermeer for instance has been the scene of violence between two of its biggest gangs, the Hopi Boys and the Kloekhorststraat Gang.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals to the KNPP include the leftist Kayan New Land Party (KNLP) and Karenni National People's Liberation Front (KNPLF), both of whom signed ceasefires with the government in the 1990s.

Colacurcio Organization.description: Criminal organization based in Seattle, Washington, founded in the 1950s by Frank Colacurcio. Engaged in racketeering, prostitution, extortion, money laundering, corruption, and fraud. Operated strip clubs as fronts for brothels and was involved in the 'Strippergate' scandal. The organization was dismantled after a 2008 raid and subsequent convictions, with the boss dying in 2010.

descriptions: Colacurcio met with a Bonanno family member to discuss a business relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: In the 1970s, Colacurcio met with Bonanno crime family member Salvatore "Bill" Bonanno in Yakima, Washington to discuss a business relationship.

Chapare Cartel.description: Bolivian criminal organization based in the Chapare region, dedicated to drug trafficking and human trafficking. It originated from the merger of six coca growers' federations and is alleged to have strong ties to the political party Movement for Socialism and former president Evo Morales. The cartel has been active since at least the 1990s, with drug trafficking increasing after the expulsion of the DEA in 2008.

descriptions: The Chapare Cartel has strong connections with the MAS political party.
edges.evidence_quote: The cartel currently has strong connections with the political party of the Movement for Socialism.

Clan Mercante-Diomede.description: Italian criminal organization based in Bari, founded by the Mercante and Diomede families. Active in drug trafficking and cigarette smuggling, operating in the Carrassi, Libertà, San Paolo, and Poggiofranco neighborhoods. Known for the 2000 San Valentino massacre targeting the rival Strisciuglio clan. Key members have been arrested and convicted.

'Ndrina Torcasio.description: The 'Ndrina Torcasio is a 'ndrina (mafia clan) of the 'Ndrangheta in Calabria, based in Lamezia Terme. It is allied with the Gualtieri, Cerra, and Giorgi-Pizzata clans. The clan has been involved in the Lamezia Terme feud since 2000, resulting in dozens of murders. In 2015, the Andromeda operation revealed a peace agreement mediated by Reggio clans.

Cártel de Guasave.description: Mexican criminal organization dedicated to drug trafficking and money laundering. It emerged in 2008 as an armed wing of the Beltrán Leyva Cartel and became independent in February 2017 under Fausto Isidro Meza Flores. Based in Guasave, Sinaloa, it is allied with La Mayiza against Los Chapitos. The group is still active.

Mazzarella clan.description: The Mazzarella clan is a powerful Camorra clan based in Naples, Italy, founded in the 1960s by the Mazzarella brothers. Initially involved in cigarette smuggling, it grew into a major criminal organization engaged in drug trafficking, money laundering, and murder. The clan has been involved in violent conflicts with rival Camorra groups and has international operations in Spain, France, and Peru.

The Yogurt Connection.description: A drug smuggling ring based in Indianapolis, Indiana, active in the late 1970s and early 1980s. The operation was valued at $50-100 million, making it the largest known ring of its kind at the time.

The group has been involved in various criminal activities including heroin smuggling and violent feuds with rival gangs. It is a member of the Kantō Hatsuka-kai federation.

'Ndrangheta.description: Italian criminal organization of mafia type originating in Calabria, recognized as a unitary criminal organization with a collegial summit. It is the only mafia present on all five continents, with a global turnover of 150 billion euros according to a 2022 DIA investigation. Active in 42 nations with 400 clans, mainly in Calabria. Its main activity is drug trafficking, followed by public contracts, vote buying, extortion, usury, arms trafficking, gambling, and toxic waste disposal.

descriptions: Sicilian Mafia helped end the Second 'Ndrangheta war and advised creating the Camera di Controllo.
edges.evidence_quote: The Sicilian Mafia contributed to the end of the conflict and probably suggested the subsequent set up of a superordinate body, similar to the Sicilian Mafia Commission

descriptions: Cosa Nostra and 'Ndrangheta have had a very close relationship, with mutual affiliations between bosses.
edges.evidence_quote: Il rapporto con la 'Ndrangheta è stato molto stretto tanto che capibastone di spicco come Antonio Macrì, Giuseppe Piromalli, Mico Tripodo (compare d'anello di Totò Riina) si affiliarono a Cosa nostra e viceversa, capi della mafia siciliana si affiliano alle 'ndrine.

descriptions: 'Ndrangheta participated in Cosa Nostra's bombing campaign in the 1990s.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel 2017 l'operazione 'Ndrangheta stragista rivelerebbe un primo coinvolgimento della 'ndrangheta nelle operazioni stragiste degli anni '90 di Cosa nostra.

descriptions: Cosa Nostra buys drugs from 'Ndrangheta.
edges.evidence_quote: Dal XXI secolo si registra un declino dell'organizzazione siciliana rispetto a quella di origine campana e calabrese, il procuratore di Catanzaro Nicola Gratteri ha più volte affermato questa tendenza, tanto da dichiarare, supportato dalle numerose operazioni anti-ndrangheta, che ora Cosa nostra messinese si rifornisce dalle 'ndrine per l'acquisto di droga

descriptions: Cosa Nostra and 'Ndrangheta cooperate in drug trafficking and waste management.
edges.evidence_quote: A novembre 2017 il neoprocuratore nazionale antimafia Federico Cafiero De Raho conferma il rapporto tra le due organizzazioni criminali non solo nel campo degli stupefacenti, ma anche nell'ambito del settore dei rifiuti e della bonifica

descriptions: 'Ndrangheta members provided support to Cosa Nostra boss Matteo Messina Denaro while he was a fugitive.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 23 marzo 2018 l'Espresso rivela che il latitante Matteo Messina Denaro avrebbe avuto appoggi da esponenti della 'ndrangheta in Toscana

descriptions: Cosa Nostra regent Nunzio Serio relied on 'Ndrangheta member Emanuele Cosentino to import drugs from Calabria.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel 2025, nell'ambito dell'operazione del Comando provinciale dei Carabinieri di Palermo ... è emerso che Nunzio Serio, reggente del mandamento di Tommaso Natale, faceva riferimento ad Emanuele Cosentino, esponente della 'ndrina Gallico di Palmi, per importare grandi quantitativi di cocaina e hashish dalla Calabria in Sicilia.

descriptions: The text groups Sicilian Mafia, 'Ndrangheta, and Camorra as major Italian criminal organizations structured around families.
edges.evidence_quote: In addition to the traditional families of the Sicilian Mafia, other major Italian criminal organizations—particularly the 'Ndrangheta in Calabria and the Camorra in Campania, are also structured around criminal families.

descriptions: Cosa Nostra yielded the drug trade to 'Ndrangheta.
edges.evidence_quote: By the late 1990s, the weakened Cosa Nostra had to yield most of the illegal drug trade to the 'Ndrangheta crime organization from Calabria.

descriptions: 'Ndrangheta surpassed Cosa Nostra as a more reliable partner for international drug trafficking organizations.
edges.evidence_quote: Cosa nostra aveva ormai perso la leadership nel traffico mondiale di droga a vantaggio della 'ndrangheta calabrese , ritenuta un partner più affidabile dalle organizzazioni internazionali del narcotraffico.
